items.title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day
items.description: Learn the 5 AI agent skills I use every day to steer Claude Code and dramatically improve code quality. Master process-driven ...
extracted_patterns: Create short, high-impact skills (3 sentences) that encode strict workflows rather than long documentation
Use 'grill me' pattern: interview the AI relentlessly about every aspect of the plan before implementation
Walk design trees - resolve all decision branches and dependencies before committing code
Explore codebase contextually during planning (not after) to answer design questions
Invoke skills at specific workflow stages (e.g., 'grill me' before 'write PRD' before implementation)
Reach shared understanding with the AI through question-driven conversation (15-40+ questions is typical for complex features)
Design skills to force specific conversation patterns that steer agents down the right path
Build multiple single-purpose skills rather than one monolithic process guide
Remember that agents have no memory - process encoded in skills becomes the guardrail

items.title: How to Use Claude Code the Boris Way
items.description: Anthropic published their official best practices for Claude Code, and Boris Cherny, the creator of Claude Code, recently shared ...
extracted_patterns: Check context window regularly with `/context` â€” context fills fast and degrades performance
Run 5-10 parallel Claude Code sessions for different tasks â€” avoids context pollution and keeps each session focused
Use Git worktrees/checkouts for parallel sessions on different branches simultaneously
4-phase workflow: (1) Explore in Plan Mode, (2) Create detailed plan, (3) Iterate on plan, (4) Implement with plan as guide
Enter Plan Mode (Shift+Tab+Tab) for all complex tasks â€” mandatory before implementation
Skip planning for trivial tasks (can describe diff in one sentence) â€” go straight to implementation
Switch to auto-accept (Shift+Tab) after solidifying plan â€” Claude executes single-shot without mid-course corrections
Give Claude verification criteria (test cases, visual verification, root cause fixing) â€” offloads feedback loop from user
Use Chrome extension for UI verification â€” Claude iterates until UI looks right and functions correctly
Use the 'go fix pattern' â€” let Claude fix most bugs automatically
Separate research/exploration from implementation into distinct phases â€” prevents context waste
Manual summarization beats auto-compact for context preservation
Treat AI as schedulable capacity â€” run multiple independent sessions, not one long conversation

items.title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies
items.description: Here is everything you need to know to crush building anything with Claude Code! These are the strategies used by top engineers ...
extracted_patterns: Global rules with CLAUDE.md files for consistent behaviors across projects
Permission management - Configure what operations are allowed automatically
Custom slash commands - Create agentic workflows executable with single commands
Context engineering with PRP framework - 3-step process for production-ready AI output
Sub-agents - Spawn specialized agent instances for parallel or focused work
Hooks for automation - Trigger automations at different points in development lifecycle
GitHub CLI integration - Manage issues/PRs directly, auto-create PRs to fix issues
YOLO mode in containers - Run with dangerous permissions safely in isolated environment
Parallel agent development - Multiple Claude Code instances on same feature simultaneously

items.title: 1000+ hours of Learning Claude Code in 10 Minutes (2026 Tutorial)
items.description: Claude Code completely changed how I build software. Try out Claude Code on Max Plan: http://clau.de/sajjaad12 Over the last 8 ...
extracted_patterns: Strategic model selection: Use Opus 4.5 (planning, ambiguous tasks) â†’ then switch to Sonnet 4.5 (execution, shipping code)
Plan-then-build workflow: Run /model opus for project design, then switch to /model sonnet to execute the plan
Treat Claude Code as a teammate, not a chatbot â€” enables collaborative workflow instead of reactive responses
Layer-based mastery pyramid: Foundation â†’ Model selection â†’ Team rules â†’ Debugging â†’ [layers 5-7 cut off]
Use IDE integrations (VS Code, PyCharm) or terminal `claude` command for flexibility
Reuse prompts via saved templates instead of retyping elaborate test cases each time

items.title: Claude Code 2.0 Has Arrived (It’s Insane)
items.description: Build agentic systems that run your business: https://skool.com/scrapes Don't miss the next build ...
extracted_patterns: Use /loop for short-term recurring tasks (within current session): check inboxes, watch for new content, track sprint changes
Use /schedule for long-term permanent automation (daily/weekly/hourly): runs in fresh instances, ideal for morning checks or regular reports
Combine scheduled tasks with skills: leverage skill descriptions to auto-select tools (e.g., YouTube tool + content repurposing skill)
Set one-off reminders with: in [time] remind me to [task]
Distinguish by duration: /loop (hours/days), /schedule (weeks/indefinitely)
Use natural language for scheduling: /loop every 10 minutes [prompt] or /schedule daily 9:00am [prompt]
Chain multiple skills in scheduled tasks: single prompt can trigger sequential skill execution
Monitor active loops via scheduled tasks document or CronList

items.title: His Claude Code Workflow Is Insane
items.description: I met Boris Cherny, the creator of Claude Code, and asked him about his personal setup. He told me he'd posted about it publicly.
extracted_patterns: Run 5 parallel Claude instances in terminal with numbered tabs (1-5), use system notifications for input awareness
Rename terminal tabs with project names to maintain mental context (e.g., 'local_anime', 'push_to_prod_app')
Use hotkeys to switch between tabs: Cmd+2/3/4 for tabs, Cmd+Left/Right for navigation within tabs
Run 5-10 web Claude sessions in parallel on claude.ai/code alongside local instances
Hand off sessions between local terminal and web interface based on task context
Use /teleport feature to sync sessions between local and web, plus mobile via Claude iOS app
Start multiple Claude sessions from iOS app each morning and check in throughout the day
Always enable extended thinking with Opus 4.5 for all coding work (requires less steering than Sonnet, better tool use)
Cloud Code works well out of boxâ€”customize only what you need for your workflow

items.title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)
items.description: This is my 50 Claude Code tips from 6 months of daily use personally and at Meta as a Staff Software Engineer. I've been coding ...
extracted_patterns: Run Claude Code from project root directory to optimize initial context zipping and token usage
Execute `/init` slash command to auto-generate CLAUDE.md from codebase analysis
Adopt pair programming mode as primary workflow (12+ hours daily active collaboration)
Prioritize code review over code writing - read every generated line of code
Use slash commands for project initialization and analysis automation
Configure build/validation loops within CLAUDE.md for automated verification
Leverage CLAUDE.md hierarchy (project-level checked-in + global ~/.claude/ rules)
Keep CLAUDE.md concise (~300 lines max) to minimize token bloat and improve AI focus
Structure CLAUDE.md with: high-level architecture, requirements, domain context, file paths, design patterns, build flows
Use memory hierarchy system to separate project-specific and user-level configuration
Minimize initial context window bloat to prevent degradation in AI instruction-following
Include keyboard shortcuts setup for workflow efficiency
Use the `claw` tool for deep project analysis and advanced workflows

items.title: Claude Code Workflows That Will 10x Your Productivity
items.description: Half my job isn't actually coding. It's writing status updates, PR descriptions, documentation, and handoff docs. I hated it, so I ...
extracted_patterns: Use Claude Code's full session context to auto-generate PR descriptions without re-prompting
Analyze git logs and commit history during on-call incidents to identify root causes quickly
Store architecture explanations in local MD-file memory system to eliminate repeated re-explanations
Feed Claude Code with daily work context (PRs, commits, decisions) to build continuously-improving domain memory
Generate status updates directly from Claude Code session context instead of manual copy-paste from chat
Leverage local execution environment to build custom automation workflows (custom 'racks')
Shift from iterative chat prompting to integrated workflows where context flows from work to automation
Maintain persistent decision/architecture logs in MD files for future investigations and documentation

items.title: How I ACTUALLY Use Claude Code... My Complete Workflow
items.description: Learn how to use Claude Code in this detailed Claude Code tutorial. We'll cover Claude Code setup, compare Claude Code vs ...
extracted_patterns: Chain specialized subagents in sequence - let each agent focus on one task (e.g., UX researcher â†’ sprint prioritizer â†’ UI designer)
Use parallel subagent execution - multiple agents work simultaneously on independent tasks to accelerate workflows
Main session acts as controller/orchestrator - distributes work to specialized agents and aggregates results
Build context before coding - create doc structure â†’ UX research â†’ sprint planning BEFORE implementation agents
Leverage 200k token windows per agent - each subagent gets full project context in isolation, reducing errors vs. single session
Prompt chain multiple agents in single workflow - trigger consecutive agents with one prompt (one prompt â†’ UX agent + sprint prioritizer both execute)
Return only essential info from agents to main session - agents do deep work, summarize for controller
Use verbose mode to observe agent behavior - enables debugging and understanding of multi-agent workflows
Structure projects with doc scaffolds before implementation - varies by project type (mapped in initial prompt)

items.title: Building a REAL feature with Claude Code: every step explained
items.description: In this video, I walk through a real-world example of using Claude Code with my course video manager. I'll show you my entire ...
extracted_patterns: Treat Claude Code like a team member - delegate with clear architecture and good feedback loops
Understand project lifecycle first - review commit history, closed issues, and codebase state before starting
Refine vague requirements through exploration - road test ideas before hardening specifications
Work on well-tested code first for lower risk and faster iteration
Avoid excessive upfront planning - follow agile/iterative approach
Break work into small, concrete enhancements rather than large features
Understand tech stack deeply (frameworks, ORMs, test setup, deployment model) before making changes
Use local development with fast iteration loops (start scripts, build commands)
Focus on architecture decisions and feedback mechanisms over implementation details
Show practical work vs. philosophy - demonstrate Claude Code in real projects with actual context

items.title: The ONLY Claude Code Tutorial You'll Ever Need in 2026
items.description: The ONLY Claude Code Tutorial You'll Ever Need in 2026. Join our FREE community with all our prompts: ...
extracted_patterns: Use Claude Code inside VS Code (files left, code middle, Claude right) for real-time visibility of all changes
Always use Planning Mode before building - let Claude ask clarifying questions and generate a plan before writing code
Model selection strategy: Opus 4.6 for complex tasks/architecture/debugging, Sonnet 4.6 for quick edits and routine tasks
Create and reuse Skills (skill.md files in .claude/skills/) as instruction templates for repetitive workflows
Use /plugin command to browse and install skills from the marketplace
Open a project folder in VS Code before starting - Claude can read, create, and edit files within that folder
Incremental build workflow: plan â†’ create files â†’ verify in browser

items.title: How to use Claude Code FREE Forever (Openrouter)
items.description: ALL Systems: https://bit.ly/4kol0y5 FREE Resources: https://bit.ly/4bfP0JR 🎙️ My Goofy pod: https://bit.ly/46nLQ3U.
extracted_patterns: Use OpenRouter as model provider to access Claude agentic framework across different model engines
Select models based on use case: Opus/Sonnet for client work, cheaper models for personal/exploratory projects
Swap model 'engines' without losing agentic features (loops, skills, orchestration) â€” framework stays the same
Evaluate cost-performance tradeoffs: Gemini Flash at 10% cost offers ~85% of Opus performance
Avoid free models for client/critical projects due to rate limiting, but use them for prototyping/learning
Cloud-based execution eliminates infrastructure requirements â€” no local GPU/RAM needed for complex workflows
